title: IoCT Services
service_type: Environmental Monitoring and IoT Data Collection
description: A hybrid IoT data collection service combining custom-developed low-power sensor nodes (developed by KIK-IRPA) with commercial off-the-shelf solutions. Custom nodes are based on the Seeedstudio Xiao ESP32-S3 and Arduino Nano RP2040 Connect platforms, integrated with sensors including DHT22 (temperature, humidity), Adafruit TSL2591 (light), Sensirion SCD30 (CO2), Sensirion SPS30 (particulate matter) and various rain gauges (DFRobot tipping bucket, Hydreon RG-15 optical). Nodes are powered by LiPo batteries with DFRobot Solar Power Manager and include on-board flash memory for local data backup. Wireless communication uses built-in WiFi/Bluetooth expanded with LoRa/LoRaWAN transceivers (Wio-SX1262 or RFM95W). Also integrates commercial sensors for temperature, humidity and moisture content in exterior walls and surrounding soil, and standard on-site meteorological weather stations. All data streams transmitted to a cloud-based platform via RESTful services (Artemis IoT Microservices) and MQTT protocol. Firmware developed in Arduino IDE by KIK-IRPA.

access_points.notes: Artemis IoT Microservices RESTful endpoints for data transmission to the cloud-based platform.
MQTT protocol for efficient, low-latency publish-subscribe communication across all data sources.

intended_for: Heritage building managers and conservation scientists requiring real-time, high-fidelity environmental monitoring of heritage assets
RHDT systems requiring a continuous, multi-variable environmental data stream as input for predictive and simulation services

used_for.activity_label: operation_01 — Real-time IoT Environmental Monitoring: continuously acquiring indoor environmental data (temperature, RH, light levels, dust particles, CO2) and outdoor meteorological data from the Japanese Tower

used_physical_object_of_type: Custom KIK-IRPA IoT nodes (Seeedstudio Xiao ESP32-S3 and Arduino Nano RP2040 Connect)
Commercial off-the-shelf temperature and humidity sensors
Commercial sensors for moisture content in exterior walls and surrounding soil
has_functionality: Measurement of indoor air temperature and surface temperatures
Measurement of light levels (visual, infrared, ultraviolet)
Measurement of wind-driven rain (WDR) on facades
Measurement of moisture content in exterior walls and surrounding soil
Standard meteorological data acquisition (temperature, RH, rainfall, wind direction, wind speed, solar radiation)
Local on-board data backup via flash memory
Low-power wireless data transmission via LoRa/LoRaWAN
Cloud data transmission via RESTful API and MQTT
